text_excerpt: Questions for written answer – Baroness Jones of Moulsecoomb Skip to content Baroness Jones of Moulsecoomb Menu Home House of Lords My Bills My Debates My Questions for Written Answer Legislation Crime and policing Employment Rights Football Governance Passenger Railway Services (Public Ownership) Planning and Infrastructure Renters’ Rights Sustainable Aviation Fuel Sentencing Water (Special Measures) Media Reports and essays Podcasts Blog Civil liberties Climate Change Ecology and animal protection Lords reform and PR National insecurity Other issues About Jenny Appointments A Day in the Life Biography In the News Jenny’s priorities Lords Diary Picture gallery Questions for written answer Peers can question the Government through by tabling questions for written answer. The questions Jenny has asked, and the answers she has received, can be viewed on the very useful ‘they work for you’ website here Peers enter questions on the Order Paper via the Lords Table Office. Peers may table up to six questions each day and can expect an answer within 14 days There is a cost to the tax payer which in 2009 was roughly £100 per question but it is closer to £800 today Share this: Share on X (Opens in new window) X Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Like Loading...
